metadata.name: winacl
metadata.summary: ACL/ACE/Security Descriptor manipulation library in pure Python

functions.name: winacl.dtyp.wcee.keyblobselector
documentation: ECDH and ECDSA structures are basically the same, so we can use the same class for both

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_DACL_AUTO_INHERIT_REQ
documentation: Indicates a required security descriptor in which the discretionary access control list (DACL) is set up to support automatic propagation of inheritable access control entries (ACEs) to existing child objects.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_DACL_AUTO_INHERITED
documentation: Indicates a security descriptor in which the discretionary access control list (DACL) is set up to support automatic propagation of inheritable access control entries (ACEs) to existing child objects.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_DACL_DEFAULTED
documentation: Indicates a security descriptor with a default DACL. For example, if the creator an object does not specify a DACL, the object receives the default DACL from the access token of the creator. This flag can affect how the system treats the DACL with respect to ACE inheritance. The system ignores this flag if the SE_DACL_PRESENT flag is not set.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_DACL_PRESENT
documentation: Indicates a security descriptor that has a DACL. If this flag is not set, or if this flag is set and the DACL is NULL, the security descriptor allows full access to everyone.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_DACL_PROTECTED
documentation: Prevents the DACL of the security descriptor from being modified by inheritable ACEs. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorControl function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_GROUP_DEFAULTED
documentation: Indicates that the security identifier (SID) of the security descriptor group was provided by a default mechanism. This flag can be used by a resource manager to identify objects whose security descriptor group was set by a default mechanism. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorGroup function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_OWNER_DEFAULTED
documentation: Indicates that the SID of the owner of the security descriptor was provided by a default mechanism. This flag can be used by a resource manager to identify objects whose owner was set by a default mechanism. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorOwner function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_RM_CONTROL_VALID
documentation: Indicates that the resource manager control is valid.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SACL_AUTO_INHERIT_REQ
documentation: Indicates a required security descriptor in which the system access control list (SACL) is set up to support automatic propagation of inheritable ACEs to existing child objects.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SACL_AUTO_INHERITED
documentation: Indicates a security descriptor in which the system access control list (SACL) is set up to support automatic propagation of inheritable ACEs to existing child objects.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SACL_DEFAULTED
documentation: A default mechanism, rather than the original provider of the security descriptor, provided the SACL. This flag can affect how the system treats the SACL, with respect to ACE inheritance. The system ignores this flag if the SE_SACL_PRESENT flag is not set. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorSacl function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SACL_PRESENT
documentation: Indicates a security descriptor that has a SACL. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorSacl function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SACL_PROTECTED
documentation: Prevents the SACL of the security descriptor from being modified by inheritable ACEs. To set this flag, use the SetSecurityDescriptorControl function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.security_descriptor.SE_SACL.SE_SELF_RELATIVE
documentation: Indicates a self-relative security descriptor. If this flag is not set, the security descriptor is in absolute format. For more information, see Absolute and Self-Relative Security Descriptors.

methods.name: winacl.dtyp.wcee.cryptoapikey.CryptoAPIKeyProperties.__init__
documentation: after DPAPI decryption, the properties are stored in a list of CryptoAPIKeyProperty objects

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.acl.ACL_REVISION.NO_DS
documentation: When set to 0x02, only AceTypes 0x00, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x11, 0x12, and 0x13 can be present in the ACL. An AceType of 0x11 is used for SACLs but not for DACLs. For more information about ACE types, see section 2.4.4.1.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.acl.ACL_REVISION.DS
documentation: When set to 0x04, AceTypes 0x05, 0x06, 0x07, 0x08, and 0x11 are allowed. ACLs of revision 0x04 are applicable only to directory service objects. An AceType of 0x11 is used for SACLs but not for DACLs.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.ADMINS
documentation: A local group used for administration of the domain.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.USERS
documentation: A local group that represents all users in the domain.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.GUESTS
documentation: A local group that represents guests of the domain.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.POWER_USERS
documentation: A local group used to represent a user or set of users who expect to treat a system as if it were their personal computer rather than as a workstation for multiple users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.ACCOUNT_OPS
documentation: A local group that exists only on systems running server operating systems. This local group permits control over nonadministrator accounts.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.SYSTEM_OPS
documentation: A local group that exists only on systems running server operating systems. This local group performs system administrative functions, not including security functions. It establishes network shares, controls printers, unlocks workstations, and performs other operations.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.PRINT_OPS
documentation: A local group that exists only on systems running server operating systems. This local group controls printers and print queues.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.BACKUP_OPS
documentation: A local group used for controlling assignment of file backup-and-restore privileges.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.REPLICATOR
documentation: A local group responsible for copying security databases from the primary domain controller to the backup domain controllers. These accounts are used only by the system.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.RAS_SERVERS
documentation: A local group that represents RAS and IAS servers. This group permits access to various attributes of user objects.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.PREW2KCOMPACCESS
documentation: A local group that exists only on systems running Windows 2000 Server. For more information, see Allowing Anonymous Access.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.REMOTE_DESKTOP_USERS
documentation: A local group that represents all remote desktop users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.NETWORK_CONFIGURATION_OPS
documentation: A local group that represents the network configuration.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.INCOMING_FOREST_TRUST_BUILDERS
documentation: A local group that represents any forest trust users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.MONITORING_USERS
documentation: A local group that represents all users being monitored.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.LOGGING_USERS
documentation: A local group responsible for logging users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.AUTHORIZATIONACCESS
documentation: A local group that represents all authorized access.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.TS_LICENSE_SERVERS
documentation: A local group that exists only on systems running server operating systems that allow for terminal services and remote access.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.DCOM_USERS
documentation: A local group that represents users who can use Distributed Component Object Model (DCOM).

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.IUSERS
documentation: A local group that represents Internet users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.CRYPTO_OPERATORS
documentation: A local group that represents access to cryptography operators.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.CACHEABLE_PRINCIPALS_GROUP
documentation: A local group that represents principals that can be cached.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.NON_CACHEABLE_PRINCIPALS_GROUP
documentation: A local group that represents principals that cannot be cached.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.EVENT_LOG_READERS_GROUP
documentation: A local group that represents event log readers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.CERTSVC_DCOM_ACCESS_GROUP
documentation: The local group of users who can connect to certification authorities using Distributed Component Object Model (DCOM).

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.RDS_REMOTE_ACCESS_SERVERS
documentation: A local group that represents RDS remote access servers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.RDS_ENDPOINT_SERVERS
documentation: A local group that represents endpoint servers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.RDS_MANAGEMENT_SERVERS
documentation: A local group that represents management servers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.HYPER_V_ADMINS
documentation: A local group that represents hyper-v admins

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.ACCESS_CONTROL_ASSISTANCE_OPS
documentation: A local group that represents access control assistance OPS.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.REMOTE_MANAGEMENT_USERS
documentation: A local group that represents remote management users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.DEFAULT_ACCOUNT
documentation: A local group that represents the default account.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.STORAGE_REPLICA_ADMINS
documentation: A local group that represents storage replica admins.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_ALIAS_RID.DEVICE_OWNERS
documentation: A local group that represents can make settings expected for Device Owners.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.ADMINS
documentation: The domain administrators' group. This account exists only on systems running server operating systems.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.USERS
documentation: A group that contains all user accounts in a domain. All users are automatically added to this group.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.GUESTS
documentation: The guest-group account in a domain.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.COMPUTERS
documentation: The domain computers' group. All computers in the domain are members of this group.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.CONTROLLERS
documentation: The domain controllers' group. All DCs in the domain are members of this group.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.CERT_ADMINS
documentation: The certificate publishers' group. Computers running Certificate Services are members of this group.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.ENTERPRISE_READONLY_DOMAIN_CONTROLLERS
documentation: The group of enterprise read-only domain controllers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.SCHEMA_ADMINS
documentation: The schema administrators' group. Members of this group can modify the Active Directory schema.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.ENTERPRISE_ADMINS
documentation: The enterprise administrators' group. Members of this group have full access to all domains in the Active Directory forest. Enterprise administrators are responsible for forest-level operations such as adding or removing new domains.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.READONLY_CONTROLLERS
documentation: The group of read-only domain controllers.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.sid.DOMAIN_GROUP_RID.CLONEABLE_CONTROLLERS
documentation: The group of cloneable domain controllers.

functions.name: winacl.functions.defines.RaiseIfZero
documentation:
Error checking for most Win32 API calls.

The function is assumed to return an integer, which is C{0} on error.
In that case the C{WindowsError} exception is raised.

functions.name: winacl.functions.defines.RaiseIfNotZero
documentation:
Error checking for some odd Win32 API calls.

The function is assumed to return an integer, which is zero on success.
If the return value is nonzero the C{WindowsError} exception is raised.

This is mostly useful for free() like functions, where the return value is
the pointer to the memory block on failure or a C{NULL} pointer on success.

functions.name: winacl.functions.defines.RaiseIfNotErrorSuccess
documentation:
Error checking for Win32 Registry API calls.

The function is assumed to return a Win32 error code. If the code is not
C{ERROR_SUCCESS} then a C{WindowsError} exception is raised.

functions.name: winacl.functions.defines.MakeANSIVersion
winacl.functions.defines.MakeWideVersion

classes.name: winacl.functions.defines.Structure
documentation: Automatically disable padding of structs and unions on 32 bits.

classes.name: winacl.functions.defines.DefaultStringType

class_variables.name: winacl.functions.constants.SE_OBJECT_TYPE.SE_WINDOW_OBJECT
documentation: Indicates a window station or desktop object on the local computer

class_variables.name: winacl.functions.constants.SE_OBJECT_TYPE.SE_DS_OBJECT
documentation: Indicates a directory service object or a property set or property of a directory service object.

class_variables.name: winacl.functions.constants.SE_OBJECT_TYPE.SE_DS_OBJECT_ALL
documentation: Indicates a directory service object and all of its property sets and properties.

class_variables.name: winacl.functions.constants.SE_OBJECT_TYPE.SE_REGISTRY_WOW64_32KEY
documentation: Indicates an object for a registry entry under WOW64.

class_variables.name: winacl.functions.constants.SE_OBJECT_TYPE.SE_REGISTRY_WOW64_64KEY
documentation: Indicates an object for a registry entry under WOW64.

functions.name: winacl.functions.netapi32.NetUserGetLocalGroups
documentation: flags can be 0 or LG_INCLUDE_INDIRECT to include all indirect group memberships

functions.name: winacl.functions.winregistry.get_nameserver_candidates
documentation:
Returns a list of IP addresses which potentially an active DNS server used by the current machine.
Please be aware that it's not at all precise!

variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.acetype2ace
documentation:
ACEType.ACCESS_ALLOWED_COMPOUND_ACE_TYPE : ,# reserved
ACEType.SYSTEM_ALARM_OBJECT_ACE_TYPE : , # reserved
ACEType.SYSTEM_ALARM_CALLBACK_ACE_TYPE : ,# reserved
ACEType.SYSTEM_ALARM_CALLBACK_OBJECT_ACE_TYPE : ,# reserved

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.CREATE_CHILD
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies a type of child object. The ACE controls the trustee's right to create this type of child object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.DELETE_CHILD
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies a type of child object. The ACE controls the trustee's right to delete this type of child object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.SELF
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies a validated write.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.READ_PROP
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies a property set or property of the object. The ACE controls the trustee's right to read the property or property set.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.WRITE_PROP
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies a property set or property of the object. The ACE controls the trustee's right to write the property or property set.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.DELETE_TREE
documentation: The right to delete all children of this object, regardless of the permissions of the children.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.LIST_OBJECT
documentation: The right to list a particular object. For more information about this right, see the see the Controlling Object Visibility article.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.CONTROL_ACCESS
documentation: The ObjectType GUID identifies an extended access right.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.DELETE
documentation: The right to delete the object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.READ_CONTROL
documentation: The right to read data from the security descriptor of the object, not including the data in the SACL.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.GENERIC_EXECUTE
documentation: The right to read permissions on, and list the contents of, a container object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.GENERIC_WRITE
documentation: The right to read permissions on this object, write all the properties on this object, and perform all validated writes to this object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.GENERIC_READ
documentation: The right to read permissions on this object, read all the properties on this object, list this object name when the parent container is listed, and list the contents of this object if it is a container.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.WRITE_DACL
documentation: The right to modify the DACL in the object security descriptor.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.WRITE_OWNER
documentation: The right to assume ownership of the object. The user must be an object trustee. The user cannot transfer the ownership to other users.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.GENERIC_ALL
documentation: The right to create or delete children, delete a subtree, read and write properties, examine children and the object itself, add and remove the object from the directory, and read or write with an extended right.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.SYNCHRONIZE
documentation: The right to use the object for synchronization. This right enables a thread to wait until that object is in the signaled state.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ADS_ACCESS_MASK.ACCESS_SYSTEM_SECURITY
documentation: The right to get or set the SACL in the object security descriptor.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_READ_DATA
documentation: For a file object, the right to read the corresponding file data. For a directory object, the right to read the corresponding directory data.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_LIST_DIRECTORY
documentation: For a directory, the right to list the contents of the directory.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_WRITE_DATA
documentation: For a file object, the right to write data to the file. For a directory object, the right to create a file in the directory (FILE_ADD_FILE).

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_APPEND_DATA
documentation: For a file object, the right to append data to the file. (For local files, write operations will not overwrite existing data if this flag is specified without FILE_WRITE_DATA.) For a directory object, the right to create a subdirectory (FILE_ADD_SUBDIRECTORY).

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_READ_EA
documentation: The right to read extended file attributes.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_WRITE_EA
documentation: The right to write extended file attributes.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_EXECUTE
documentation: For a native code file, the right to execute the file. This access right given to scripts may cause the script to be executable, depending on the script interpreter.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_DELETE_CHILD
documentation: For a directory, the right to delete a directory and all the files it contains, including read-only files.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_READ_ATTRIBUTES
documentation: The right to read file attributes.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_WRITE_ATTRIBUTES
documentation: The right to write file attributes.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.FILE_ACCESS_MASK.FILE_ALL_ACCESS
documentation: All possible access rights for a file.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.ALL_ACCESS
documentation: Includes STANDARD_RIGHTS_REQUIRED, in addition to all access rights in this table.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.CREATE_SERVICE
documentation: Required to call the CreateService function to create a service object and add it to the database.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.CONNECT
documentation: Required to connect to the service control manager.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.ENUMERATE_SERVICE
documentation: Required to call the EnumServicesStatus or EnumServicesStatusEx function to list the services that are in the database. Required to call the NotifyServiceStatusChange function to receive notification when any service is created or deleted.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.LOCK
documentation: Required to call the LockServiceDatabase function to acquire a lock on the database.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.MODIFY_BOOT_CONFIG
documentation: Required to call the NotifyBootConfigStatus function.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SC_MANAGER_ACCESS_MASK.QUERY_LOCK_STATUS
documentation: Required to call the QueryServiceLockStatus function to retrieve the lock status information for the database.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_ALL_ACCESS
documentation: Includes STANDARD_RIGHTS_REQUIRED in addition to all access rights in this table.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_CHANGE_CONFIG
documentation: Required to call the ChangeServiceConfig or ChangeServiceConfig2 function to change the service configuration. Because this grants the caller the right to change the executable file that the system runs, it should be granted only to administrators.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_ENUMERATE_DEPENDENTS
documentation: Required to call the EnumDependentServices function to enumerate all the services dependent on the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_INTERROGATE
documentation: Required to call the ControlService function to ask the service to report its status immediately.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_PAUSE_CONTINUE
documentation: Required to call the ControlService function to pause or continue the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_QUERY_CONFIG
documentation: Required to call the QueryServiceConfig and QueryServiceConfig2 functions to query the service configuration.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_QUERY_STATUS
documentation: Required to call the QueryServiceStatus or QueryServiceStatusEx function to ask the service control manager about the status of the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_START
documentation: Required to call the StartService function to start the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_STOP
documentation: Required to call the ControlService function to stop the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.SERVICE_USER_DEFINED_CONTROL
documentation: Required to call the ControlService function to specify a user-defined control code.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.DELETE
documentation: Required to call the DeleteService function to delete the service.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.READ_CONTROL
documentation: Required to call the QueryServiceObjectSecurity function to query the security descriptor of the service object.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.WRITE_DAC
documentation: Required to call the SetServiceObjectSecurity function to modify the Dacl member of the service object's security descriptor.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.SERVICE_ACCESS_MASK.WRITE_OWNER
documentation: Required to call the SetServiceObjectSecurity function to modify the Owner and Group members of the service object's security descriptor.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_ALL_ACCESS
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_REQUIRED, KEY_QUERY_VALUE, KEY_SET_VALUE, KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY, KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS, KEY_NOTIFY, and KEY_CREATE_LINK access rights.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY
documentation: Required to create a subkey of a registry key.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS
documentation: Required to enumerate the subkeys of a registry key.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_NOTIFY
documentation: Required to request change notifications for a registry key or for subkeys of a registry key.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_QUERY_VALUE
documentation: Required to query the values of a registry key.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_READ
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_READ, KEY_QUERY_VALUE, KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS, and KEY_NOTIFY values.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_SET_VALUE
documentation: Required to create, delete, or set a registry value.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_WOW64_32KEY
documentation: Indicates that an application on 64-bit Windows should operate on the 32-bit registry view. This flag is ignored by 32-bit Windows. For more information, see Accessing an Alternate Registry View. This flag must be combined using the OR operator with the other flags in this table that either query or access registry values. Windows 2000: This flag is not supported.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_WOW64_64KEY
documentation: Indicates that an application on 64-bit Windows should operate on the 64-bit registry view. This flag is ignored by 32-bit Windows. For more information, see Accessing an Alternate Registry View. This flag must be combined using the OR operator with the other flags in this table that either query or access registry values. Windows 2000: This flag is not supported.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.REGISTRY_ACCESS_MASK.KEY_WRITE
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_WRITE, KEY_SET_VALUE, and KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY access rights.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ACE_OBJECT_PRESENCE.NONE
documentation: Neither ObjectType nor InheritedObjectType are valid.

class_variables.name: winacl.dtyp.ace.ACE_OBJECT_PRESENCE.ACE_INHERITED_OBJECT_TYPE_PRESENT
documentation: InheritedObjectType is valid. If this value is not specified, all types of child objects can inherit the ACE.

modules.name: winacl.examples.serviceacl
documentation: list services current user has write access to
list services given user has write access to

functions.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.GetEffectiveRightsFromAclW
documentation: Takes a SID instead of a trustee!

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.OWNER_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The owner identifier of the object is being referenced.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.GROUP_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The primary group identifier of the object is being referenced.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.DACL_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The DACL of the object is being referenced.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.SACL_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The SACL of the object is being referenced.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.LABEL_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The mandatory integrity label is being referenced.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.UNPROTECTED_SACL_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The SACL inherits access control entries (ACEs) from the parent object.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.UNPROTECTED_DACL_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The DACL inherits ACEs from the parent object.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.BACKUP_SECURITY_INFORMATION
documentation: The security descriptor is being accessed for use in a backup operation.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_ALL_ACCESS
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_REQUIRED, KEY_QUERY_VALUE, KEY_SET_VALUE, KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY, KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS, KEY_NOTIFY, and KEY_CREATE_LINK access rights.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY
documentation: Required to create a subkey of a registry key.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS
documentation: Required to enumerate the subkeys of a registry key.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_NOTIFY
documentation: Required to request change notifications for a registry key or for subkeys of a registry key.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_QUERY_VALUE
documentation: Required to query the values of a registry key.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_READ
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_READ, KEY_QUERY_VALUE, KEY_ENUMERATE_SUB_KEYS, and KEY_NOTIFY values.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_SET_VALUE
documentation: Required to create, delete, or set a registry value.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_WOW64_32KEY
documentation: Indicates that an application on 64-bit Windows should operate on the 32-bit registry view. This flag is ignored by 32-bit Windows. For more information, see Accessing an Alternate Registry View. #This flag must be combined using the OR operator with the other flags in this table that either query or access registry values. #Windows 2000: This flag is not supported.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_WOW64_64KEY
documentation: Indicates that an application on 64-bit Windows should operate on the 64-bit registry view. This flag is ignored by 32-bit Windows. For more information, see Accessing an Alternate Registry View. This flag must be combined using the OR operator with the other flags in this table that either query or access registry values. Windows 2000: This flag is not supported.

variables.name: winacl.functions.advapi32.KEY_WRITE
documentation: Combines the STANDARD_RIGHTS_WRITE, KEY_SET_VALUE, and KEY_CREATE_SUB_KEY access rights.
